questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Face value of a share is the amount at which the share is issued.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The company records the face value of share in its books.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Face value of a share is indeed the amount at which the share is issued, and this amount is recorded in the company's books.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Premium received on issue of shares is the excess of issue price over face value.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Premium received is recorded as a part of share capital.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Premium received on issue of shares is the excess of issue price over face value, and it is recorded as a part of share capital.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Share capital is an owner's fund.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Share capital is contributed by the shareholders in the form of cash or other assets.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Share capital is indeed an owner's fund, as it is contributed by the shareholders in the form of cash or other assets.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Share capital cannot be reduced.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Share capital is a part of owner's fund.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Share capital can be reduced, and the reason is not related to it being a part of owner's fund.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Debentures are a type of loan.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Debentures are issued at a discount or at a premium.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Debentures are a type of loan, but the reason for being issued at a discount or at a premium is not related to this fact.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Calls in arrears refer to the amount due from shareholders on account of calls not made.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Calls in arrears are treated as a liability of the company.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Calls in arrears indeed refer to the amount due from shareholders on account of calls not made, and it is treated as a liability of the company.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Share capital is divided into two main types: equity share capital and preference share capital.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Preference share capital is further divided into different types.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Share capital is indeed divided into two main types: equity share capital and preference share capital, and preference share capital is further divided into different types.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Redeemable preference shares are those which are redeemed at the end of a specified period.
questions.reason: Reason (R): They are issued at a discount or at a premium.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Redeemable preference shares are indeed those which are redeemed at the end of a specified period, but the reason for being issued at a discount or at a premium is not related to this fact.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The company can issue shares at a discount.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The company can issue shares at a premium.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The company can indeed issue shares at a discount, and it is a separate reason that the company can also issue shares at a premium.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Dividend on preference shares is paid before dividend on equity shares.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Preference shareholders have a fixed rate of return.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Dividend on preference shares is indeed paid before dividend on equity shares, and it is because preference shareholders have a fixed rate of return.
